notes: DSM associations are diagnostic category references for threat modeling purposes, not diagnostic claims.
BCI relevance ratings reflect current device landscape, not theoretical potential.
Confidence ratings: HIGH = established physiology, MEDIUM = well-characterized with some gaps, INFERRED = mechanistic inference from literature.

axes.name: Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Adrenal
axes.pathway: Hypothalamus (PVN) -> CRH -> Anterior Pituitary (corticotroph cells) -> ACTH -> Adrenal Cortex (zona fasciculata) -> Cortisol

co_releasing.name: AVP
co_releasing.role: Synergistic co-secretagogue — amplifies ACTH release with CRH

receptors.name: Glucocorticoid Receptor
receptors.function: Mediates negative feedback; suppresses CRH and POMC transcription

receptors.name: Mineralocorticoid Receptor
receptors.function: Binds cortisol with higher affinity than NR3C1 at low concentrations

feedback_details.fast_negative: Minutes: cortisol -> GR and MR in pituitary and hypothalamus -> suppresses CRH and ACTH release
feedback_details.slow_negative: Hours: cortisol -> GR -> suppresses CRH and POMC gene transcription
feedback_details.short_loop: ACTH feeds back on hypothalamus to suppress CRH
feedback_details.ultradian: Coordinated GR/MR signaling generates 1-2 hour glucocorticoid pulses even at rest; disrupted in depression

axes.qif_notes: Hypothalamus/PVN is the command node (N4). Pituitary at sella turcica (N4 floor). Adrenal gland is peripheral effector (N1-equivalent distance).

dsm_conditions.association: 40-60% show hypercortisolemia, dexamethasone non-suppression, blunted CRH-ACTH response
Hypocortisolemia with enhanced negative feedback; blunted cortisol awakening response
Elevated cortisol awakening response (CAR) in both phases
Excess ACTH from pituitary adenoma; depression, anxiety, cognitive impairment, psychosis at high cortisol
Adrenal insufficiency; depression, fatigue, psychosis (adrenal crisis)
HPA hyperactivity documented; chronic antipsychotic use partially normalizes
HPA hyperreactivity
HPA hyperreactivity

axes.epigenetic_notes: Childhood trauma permanently programs HPA axis reactivity via epigenetic methylation of NR3C1 promoter (Meaney-Szyf paradigm; replications mixed).
axes.bci_notes: Hypothalamic DBS in early investigation. VNS (CN X) modulates NTS -> hypothalamus -> HPA axis, providing indirect HPA neuromodulation. Proposed mechanism for VNS antidepressant effects.

axes.name: Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Thyroid
axes.pathway: Hypothalamus (PVN/medial hypothalamus) -> TRH -> Anterior Pituitary (thyrotroph cells) -> TSH -> Thyroid follicular cells -> T4/T3

hypothalamic_hormone.name: TRH
hypothalamic_hormone.additional_roles: Also acts as a CNS neurotransmitter independent of the axis

pituitary_hormone.name: TSH
pituitary_hormone.gene: TSHB (beta subunit), CGA (alpha subunit, shared with LH, FSH, hCG)

T4.note: Major secretory product (90%). Largely a prohormone, converted to T3 peripherally.

T3.note: Biologically active form. Produced from T4 via deiodinase enzymes.

receptors.name: TSH Receptor
receptors.signaling: cAMP -> PKA -> thyroid hormone synthesis and secretion

receptors.name: Thyroid Hormone Receptor Beta

subtypes.TRb2: Specifically mediates negative feedback of T3 on TRH gene expression in hypothalamic PVN neurons

receptors.ligand_note: T3 binds 10-15x higher affinity than T4

feedback_details.pituitary_negative: T3 (and T4 converted to T3 by DIO2 in thyrotrophs) suppress TSH secretion
feedback_details.hypothalamic_negative: T3 suppresses TRH gene transcription in PVN neurons via TRb2 binding
feedback_details.short_loop: TSH has slight short-loop feedback on TRH (inferred; less well established)

cofactors: Iodine (iodide transport by SLC5A5/NIS; incorporated into thyroglobulin by TPO)
axes.qif_notes: Hypothalamus (PVN) is N4. TRH neurons in the PVN; pituitary at sella (N4/peripheral boundary).

dsm_conditions.association: Depression (often mistaken for MDD), cognitive slowing, psychosis in severe cases (myxedema madness)
Anxiety, agitation, irritability, mania-like episodes, insomnia
Depression association documented; treatment trials show mixed results
Lithium inhibits thyroid hormone release and blocks TSH signaling in up to 40% of bipolar patients on long-term lithium
Associated with postpartum depression

axes.additional_notes: TRH has independent CNS effects (antidepressant-like, analeptic) suggesting HPT dysregulation has direct neural consequences beyond metabolism.
axes.bci_notes: No direct BCI interfaces to the HPT axis. Hypothalamic neuromodulation (DBS research) could theoretically modulate TRH secretion (inferred, purely experimental).

axes.name: Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Gonadal
axes.pathway: Hypothalamus (GnRH neurons, mediobasal hypothalamus and preoptic area) -> GnRH (pulsatile) -> Anterior Pituitary (gonadotroph cells) -> LH + FSH -> Gonads -> Testosterone / Estradiol / Progesterone

hypothalamic_hormone.name: GnRH
hypothalamic_hormone.note: Continuous GnRH exposure paradoxically suppresses gonadotropins (basis for GnRH agonist therapy)

co_releasing.name: Kisspeptin
co_releasing.source: Kiss1 neurons in arcuate nucleus and AVPV
co_releasing.role: Primary gating signal for puberty onset; drives pulsatile GnRH release

male.fsh_target: Sertoli cells -> spermatogenesis + Inhibin B (selectively inhibits FSH)

female.follicular_phase: FSH -> granulosa cells -> Estradiol (via aromatase, CYP19A1)

receptors.name: GnRH Receptor
receptors.unique_feature: No C-terminal tail (unique among GPCRs); does not internalize efficiently

axes.feedback_type: negative (with positive feedback reversal in females)

feedback_details.long_loop_negative: Testosterone and estradiol suppress GnRH pulse frequency/amplitude and suppress LH/FSH release at pituitary
feedback_details.positive_feedback: High estradiol (>200 pg/mL sustained 48h) triggers LH surge via positive feedback — unique reversal of negative feedback (pre-ovulatory, females only)
feedback_details.inhibin_b: Selectively suppresses FSH (from Sertoli cells in males, granulosa cells in females)
feedback_details.positive_feedback_note: Precise GnRH neuron circuitry for estradiol-induced LH surge remains incompletely characterized (inferred)

axes.qif_notes: GnRH neurons span mediobasal hypothalamus and preoptic area (N4). Kiss1 neurons in arcuate nucleus and AVPV are key N4 nodes.

dsm_conditions.association: Depression, cognitive decline, reduced motivation. Low testosterone associated with MDD in men.
Luteal phase progesterone metabolite (allopregnanolone) acts on GABA-A receptors; abnormal sensitivity implicated
Rapid estrogen/progesterone withdrawal post-delivery
LH hypersecretion, androgen excess; high prevalence of anxiety and depression
HPG axis is the pharmacological target for gender-affirming hormone therapy
Functional hypogonadism from weight loss suppressing kisspeptin/GnRH

axes.bci_notes: No direct BCI interfaces. Hypothalamic neuromodulation affecting kisspeptin/GnRH pulse generation is theoretically possible but purely experimental.

axes.name: Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Somatotropic
axes.pathway: Hypothalamus (arcuate nucleus/PVN) -> GHRH (stimulatory) + Somatostatin (inhibitory) -> Anterior Pituitary (somatotroph cells) -> GH -> Liver (and other tissues) -> IGF-1

receptors.name: GH Receptor
receptors.mechanism: Homodimerization upon GH binding -> JAK2 phosphorylation -> STAT5b activation -> IGF-1 gene transcription in liver

feedback_details.long_loop_negative: IGF-1 inhibits GH release from pituitary (direct somatotroph inhibition) and stimulates somatostatin release from hypothalamus
feedback_details.short_loop: GH feeds back on hypothalamus to stimulate somatostatin and inhibit GHRH
feedback_details.ultradian_rhythm: GH released in pulses (every 3-4 hours), largest pulse during slow-wave sleep (N3). Somatostatin tonic inhibition between pulses.
feedback_details.ghrelin_interplay: Ghrelin suppresses somatostatin indirectly, amplifying GH pulses

axes.sleep_dependency: Disrupted slow-wave sleep architecture impairs the major nocturnal GH pulse.
axes.qif_notes: Arcuate nucleus and PVN are N4 nodes. Ghrelin signals from periphery to N4 hypothalamus.

dsm_conditions.association: Depression, cognitive impairment, reduced quality of life. GH replacement improves mood in some trials.
GH excess from pituitary adenoma; anxiety, depression, cognitive changes; sleep apnea (genioglossus hypertrophy)
Disrupted GH pulses; associated with MDD, PTSD, insomnia disorder
Ghrelin dysregulation associated with anorexia nervosa and binge eating disorder

axes.bci_notes: Indirect. Hypothalamic neuromodulation targeting slow-wave sleep enhancement could modulate GH axis pulsatility (experimental, inferred). Ghrelin receptor (GHSR) investigated as pharmacological target in eating disorders.

axes.name: Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Prolactin
axes.pathway: Hypothalamus (arcuate nucleus TIDA neurons) -> Dopamine (inhibitory) -> Anterior Pituitary (lactotroph cells) -> PRL (released when dopamine inhibition is lifted)
axes.axis_type: Tonic inhibition (unique: default state is suppression by dopamine)

hypothalamic_hormone.name: Dopamine (inhibitory)
hypothalamic_hormone.source: Tuberoinfundibular dopaminergic (TIDA) neurons in arcuate nucleus
hypothalamic_hormone.mechanism: Constitutively suppresses PRL synthesis and secretion

stimulatory_inputs.name: TRH
stimulatory_inputs.note: Also stimulates PRL release; explains elevated PRL in hypothyroidism when TRH rises

target_hormone.name: Prolactin (PRL)
target_hormone.targets: Mammary gland (milk production), hypothalamus (short-loop feedback), immune cells, liver

receptors.name: Prolactin Receptor
receptors.location: Breast, liver, kidney, adrenal, testes, ovary, immune cells, brain

axes.feedback_type: short-loop (unique: PRL inhibits its own inhibitor)

feedback_details.short_loop: PRL suppresses dopamine release from TIDA neurons (inhibits its own inhibitor -> allows sustained lactation)
feedback_details.suckling_reflex: Sensory afferents from nipple -> vagal pathway -> hypothalamus -> suppresses dopamine (and stimulates oxytocin release simultaneously)
feedback_details.stress_response: Any stress can transiently elevate PRL via reduced dopamine tone

cofactors: Vitamin B6 (pyridoxine, cofactor for AADC in dopamine synthesis)
axes.qif_notes: Arcuate nucleus TIDA neurons (N4). Dopaminergic control pathway bridges N4 to N5 (basal ganglia dopamine system interconnections).

dsm_conditions.association: Amenorrhea, galactorrhea, sexual dysfunction, depression, anxiety. Antipsychotic-induced: near-universal with typical antipsychotics (D2 antagonists), common with risperidone, amisulpride.
Benign pituitary tumor; depression, sexual dysfunction, infertility
Rapid prolactin/estrogen fluctuations implicated
All D2-blocking antipsychotics elevate PRL — relevant to monitoring

axes.bci_notes: Indirect. Dopamine modulation from central neuromodulation (DBS, VNS) could affect TIDA tone and PRL. DBS targeting substantia nigra (Parkinson's) affects broader dopamine network with downstream PRL effects (inferred).

axes.name: Neurohypophyseal System (Posterior Pituitary)
axes.pathway: Hypothalamus (magnocellular neurons in SON and PVN) -> axonal transport via supraoptico-hypophyseal tract -> Posterior Pituitary (neurohypophysis) -> systemic release of OXT and AVP
axes.axis_type: Direct neural release (not a classic three-tier axis)

hypothalamic_hormone.name: Oxytocin (OXT) and Vasopressin (AVP)
hypothalamic_hormone.source: Magnocellular neurons in supraoptic nucleus (SON) and paraventricular nucleus (PVN)

hormones.name: Oxytocin
release_triggers: Suckling (vagal afferents from nipple -> NTS -> SON/PVN)
Uterine stretch (during labor — positive feedback loop)
Dendritic OXT release during social contexts (autocrine/paracrine)

pituitary_hormone.name: OXT and AVP (released directly from posterior pituitary)

target_hormone.name: Oxytocin / Vasopressin
target_hormone.gland: Posterior pituitary (neurohypophysis) — direct release, no target gland

receptors.name: Oxytocin Receptor
receptors.location: Uterus, breast, CNS (amygdala, hippocampus, hypothalamus, brainstem), immune cells

receptors.name: Vasopressin V1a Receptor
receptors.location: Vascular smooth muscle, liver, platelets, CNS

receptors.name: Vasopressin V1b Receptor
receptors.cross_axis: Synergizes with CRH to release ACTH — bridges OXT/AVP system to HPA axis

receptors.name: Vasopressin V2 Receptor
receptors.mechanism: Mediates antidiuretic effect via aquaporin-2 (AQP2) insertion

axes.feedback_type: mixed (positive feedback for OXT during parturition; auto-inhibition for AVP)

feedback_details.apelin: Gene APLN; inversely regulated by osmotic stress; counters AVP during euhydration
feedback_details.oxt_positive_feedback: Ferguson reflex during parturition: uterine stretch -> OXT -> contraction -> more stretch (rare positive feedback loop in physiology)

axes.qif_notes: Hypothalamic SON and PVN are N4 nodes. Posterior pituitary is the terminal release site. V1bR on pituitary corticotrophs bridges N4 to HPA axis.

dsm_conditions.association: AVP deficiency; polyuria, polydipsia. Causes: trauma, surgery, tumors, autoimmune, Wolfram syndrome (WFS1 gene).
Hyponatremia -> confusion, seizures, cerebral edema. Triggered by SSRIs (mechanism uncertain), carbamazepine, antipsychotics — highly relevant psychiatric medication interaction.
Reduced OXT levels and OXTR polymorphisms documented; intranasal OXT trials ongoing (mixed results as of 2025)
OXT modulates amygdala fear responses
OXT administration shows anxiolytic effects in some trials
OXT dysregulation in context of parturition/lactation
OXT studied as adjunctive treatment for social cognition deficits

axes.bci_notes: Intranasal OXT administration demonstrates effects on social behavior, trust, and amygdala reactivity. Hypothalamic neuromodulation for OXT release is being explored. V1bR -> HPA axis connection means PVN-targeting BCI could modulate both OXT/AVP and HPA simultaneously — relevant multi-axis threat surface (inferred).

axes.name: Pineal Gland / Melatonin Synthesis
axes.pathway: Retina (ipRGCs/melanopsin) -> retinohypothalamic tract -> SCN -> PVN -> intermediolateral cell column (spinal cord) -> superior cervical ganglion -> postganglionic sympathetic fibers -> pinealocytes -> Melatonin
axes.axis_type: Neuroendocrine transducer (not a hypothalamic-pituitary axis). Converts SCN-driven photic signals into melatonin secretion.

hypothalamic_hormone.name: SCN-driven sympathetic signal (norepinephrine)
hypothalamic_hormone.source: SCN -> PVN -> superior cervical ganglion -> pinealocytes
hypothalamic_hormone.mechanism: Norepinephrine activates beta1 + alpha1 -> cAMP + Ca2+ -> AANAT phosphorylation -> melatonin synthesis

receptors.name: Melatonin Receptor 1 (MT1)
receptors.function: Circadian phase resetting, SCN signaling, vasoconstriction

receptors.name: Melatonin Receptor 2 (MT2)
receptors.gwas_note: MTNR1B variants are GWAS-associated with type 2 diabetes

feedback_details.note: Melatonin does not have a classic negative feedback loop. Regulation is primarily photic (light/dark cycle) via the SCN sympathetic pathway.
feedback_details.darkness_pathway: NE release -> beta1-AR -> cAMP -> PKA -> AANAT phosphorylation + CREB activation -> melatonin peaks (midnight-3am)
feedback_details.light_suppression: Photic signal suppresses SCN output -> no NE -> AANAT dephosphorylated and degraded by proteasome -> melatonin suppressed

key_regulatory_genes.14_3_3_note: 14-3-3 proteins stabilize phosphorylated AANAT, preventing proteasomal degradation

axes.qif_notes: SCN is an N4 node; pineal gland is epithalamic (N4/N3 junction). Retinohypothalamic tract provides direct CN II -> N4 information channel outside classical visual pathway.

dsm_conditions.association: Reduced melatonin amplitude and phase delays. Light therapy works by phase-advancing melatonin/SCN clock.
Delayed and advanced sleep-wake phase disorders directly involve AANAT/melatonin timing dysregulation
Melatonin onset is a biological marker for mood states; phase advances in mania
Melatonin supplementation has modest evidence; ramelteon (MT1/MT2 agonist) is FDA-approved
Delayed circadian phase documented; melatonin for sleep-onset insomnia in ADHD has pediatric evidence
Disrupted circadian rhythms, reduced melatonin secretion
ASMT gene variants associated with ASD in some studies

axes.bci_notes: Light therapy devices are approved interventions operating through CN II -> retinohypothalamic tract -> SCN -> pineal pathway (photic BCI at CN II/N4). Blue light from BCI screens/devices suppresses melatonin via melanopsin (OPN4) — relevant environmental threat surface.
